---
title: "Unessay"
about:
  id: me
  template: broadside
  image: img/Antoniol__20240821-_A9_0515.jpg
---

::: {#me}
## Who and what?

I am Zeb Antonioli. I am originally from Montana and am currently studying Microbiology at the University of New Hampshire. I originally got the photography bug in 2018 on a trip to New Zealand. From there, I was hooked. I have done multiple generas of photography; however, my favorites are astrophotography and wildlife photography. This unessay assignment is meant to marry my academic interests with my passion of wildlife photography. Over the course of the past 6 years, I have had the privilege of photography many different animals, and here I will present relevant papers and research that has been conducted specifically on the microbiome or portion of the microbiome of various species I have photographed. I will accompany this information with images I have taken along with a personal artist statement. I hope to return to my wildlife photography work as it has been put on the backburner due to my academics. Ideally, I will be able to persue both once I graduate. 

:::

![](img/_8500145.jpg){width="100%"}

**Weathered** — *Docked on the side of the Chobe, this lone bull slowly walked past our boat. I went on the bow and leaned as far as I could to get the head on shot. He came about 20 meters from me and just kept moving down the river indifferent to our presence. This portrait was a shot that I had envisioned for months and was so happy when I had the chance to capture it.*

![](img/MountRainierInsta2-2.jpg){width="100%"}

**Timeless** — *.*
![](img/DeathValleySelfie.jpg){width="100%"}

**Playa Selfie**

![](img/TransitRainier.jpg){width="100%"}

**Million Miles per Hour**
